raw_consumer_statement: I have a credit card with Citi bank. In XXXX fraudulent transactions started appearing on my account. The fraudulent charges were all made in Georgia ( I live in Kansas ). Citi bank allowed someone to change my billing address and email from my Kansas address to an address in Georgia and issued a card to that address. Over the course of 3 weeks, XXXX  fraudulent transactions were made totaling just shy of {$12000.00}, maxing out the credit limit on the account. I was not notified of the address change. No one bothered to flag the account when the excessive spending started. This card isn't a card I use daily, I only have a couple automated payments that hit it each month. My monthly bill over the last year plus was around {$25.00} a month. This is negligence on Citi Bank 's part to allow almost {$12000.00} in charges in three weeks on a card that typically holds {$25.00}. I have disputed all of the charges with Citi bank and it is currently under investigation but I will no longer be banking with Citi Bank since they aren't interested in protecting their customers.
